rulings.Case Title: JAMES MARZO ET AL VS. CORRESE DI GRAZIA ARTIERES ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday May 14, 2018, line 4. PLAINTIFF JAMES MARZO, JAMES MARZO DESIGN, INC. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Injunction continued to June 15, 2018 per May 4, 2018 Order. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: FREDERICK HUTCHINS MOLL VS. JUDITH M BRASS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Temporary Restraining Order And Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday May 14, 2018, line 5. Temporary Restraining Order And Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction DENIED. Moving party failed to sustain their burden. There is no evidence that the conduct Plaintiffs going to continue. Past harm may be compensated by money damages. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday May 14, 2018, line 6. DEFENDANT CHI TRAN MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, UNLAWFUL DETAINER GRANTED, subject to opposition. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: RITA Q LAU VS. CHI TRAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings As To Defendant'S Amended Answer To Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, May 14, 2018, Line 6. PLAINTIFF RITA LAU'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings As To Defendant's Amended Answer To Complaint. moot in light of ruling on Motion for Summary Judgment. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: MERCY HOUSING CALIFORNIA VS. CHELENA STARKS-CHURCH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Set Aside Dismissal And Grant Mtn For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, May 14, 2018, Line 7. DEFENDANT CHELENA STARKS-CHURCH'S Motion To Set Aside Dismissal And Grant Mtn For Summary Judgment. DENIED. Dismissal filed prior to deadline to present an opposition, which in unlawful detainer action may be made orally at the time of the hearing. See Zapanta v. Unversal Care Inc. (2003) 107 Cal.App.4th 1167. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: SOPHIA CHEN VS. STEVE SVIENTY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, May 14, 2018, Line 8. DEFENDANT STEVE SVIENTY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint sufficiently states a cause of action for unlawful detainer and is not uncertain. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: KIM KLAUSNER VS. AIMEE TSAO ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, May 14, 2018, Line 9. DEFENDANT AIMEE TSAO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to reconcile its allegations in the Notice (showing noncompliance with SFRO requirements for Relative Move-In) and Paragraph 14 of the complaint (Plaintiff has met all applicable requirements of the ordinance). If Plaintiff wishes to make a constitutional challenge to SFRO 37.9(a)(8) (as basis for not complying with SFRO), Plaintiff shall so allege, if such challenge is proper within an unlawful detainer action. =(501/REQ)
